title: frangula scopulorum
intro: Frangula scopulorum, also known as the dwarf buckthorn or Rocky Mountain buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its thorny branches and yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of F. scopulorum lie in its bark, which contains anthraquinones, compounds that have been used to treat various gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of F. scopulorum include relief from constipation, diarrhea, and other digestive problems, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Frangula scopulorum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal purposes, although proper dosage and preparation methods should be consulted with a healthcare professional.
